seoTitle: How to Hide Your Real Name on PayPal (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to shield your legal name on PayPal by switching to a Business profile with a custom display name and tighter search privacy settings.
intro: PayPal's personal accounts show your real legal name to anyone you pay or get paid by, which isn't ideal if you value privacy. This guide walks through upgrading to a Business profile so you can display a custom name or alias instead. You'll learn how to adjust search privacy and invoicing so your legal identity stays out of everyday transactions.

steps.heading: Understand why your name shows
steps.body: On a standard Personal account, PayPal's network requires your real first and last name to appear to anyone you transact with, since this ties into KYC compliance rules.

steps.heading: Start the Business account upgrade
steps.body: From your account Settings, find the option to upgrade to a Business profile, then choose whether to convert your existing account or create a separate one for private activity.

steps.heading: Set a custom display name
steps.body: During setup, enter a business alias or brand name that will show at checkout instead of your legal name.

steps.heading: Adjust search privacy settings
steps.body: Under Data & Privacy, open Search Privacy and turn off options that let others find your profile via your real name or phone number.

steps.heading: Check invoicing and test a payment
steps.body: Review your invoice templates so your legal details don't leak to customers, then send a small test payment to confirm only your business name appears in the transaction history.

faq.question: Will switching to a Business account cost anything?
faq.answer: PayPal Business accounts don't have a signup fee, though standard payment processing fees still apply to transactions.

faq.question: Can I switch back to a Personal account later?
faq.answer: PayPal allows account type changes, but it's easier to plan ahead since some settings and history may need to be reconfigured.

faq.question: Does hiding my name affect tax reporting?
faq.answer: No, PayPal still tracks your legal identity on the backend for tax and compliance purposes even if your display name is hidden from other users.

faq.question: Will my bank withdrawals still work with a business alias?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as your linked bank account details match your legal registration on file, withdrawals will process normally.
